text: Isolation of BMDMs: After biosafety cabinet disinfection, C57 mice were anesthetized, and the femur region was sterilized. Femur and tibia were isolated, disinfected, and bone marrow was flushed out with D-PBS. The suspension was filtered through a 40 μm strainer and washed. Cells were collected by centrifugation at 800 rpm for 5 minutes and washed twice with D-PBS. Bone marrow mononuclear cells were resuspended in DMEM complete medium containing M-CSF, seeded in 12-well plates, and incubated at 37°C. After 24 hours, non-adherent cells were removed by changing the medium, and subsequent medium changes were performed every other day. After 7 days, differentiated macrophages were obtained.
